task: I was at work and noticed a wallet sitting in a cart. I want to make sure it gets handled the right way before the shift gets busy. What’s the best way to go about that?

turns.agent_response:
Best move: turn it in right away through the normal lost-and-found or supervisor process.

Simple steps:
- Don’t go through it unless your workplace specifically requires checking for ID.
- Take it to a manager, supervisor, or customer service desk.
- Say where you found it and about what time.
- If there’s a standard lost-and-found log, have them note it.
- If your job has a policy for found valuables, follow that exactly.

If you want to be extra careful, keep it in sight until you hand it off so it doesn’t get misplaced.

If you tell me what kind of workplace it is, I can help you figure out the most likely right person to give it to.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s the right move. Hand it off right away, say where you found it, and let them log it if they do that kind of thing. I wouldn’t start digging through it unless the place has a rule for checking ID. Keep it simple and clean so nobody can say it got handled wrong. If it’s busy, I’d just take it straight to the supervisor or front desk and be done with it.
